channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.gamedeveloper.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.gamedeveloper.com

item.title: New publisher Trailmark Games promises 'new path forward' for licensed games
item.content:encoded:
Image via Trailmark Games

Newsbrief: Former GameMill Entertainment executives, responsible for the likes of Nickelodeon All-Star Brawl and Cobra Kai, have formally announced their new publishing company, Trailmark Games. The company claims to have a "new path forward" for licensed games.

The publisher has four games in the works, including The Walking Dead: Streets of Survival, where Rick Grimes, Daryl Dixon, and Michonne from AMC's The Walking Dead TV series are playable characters.

Trailmark Games is described as an "IP-driven video game publisher," and the company has the rights to "a portfolio of established titles and distribution rights." That includes Big Buck Hunter: Ultimate Trophy, Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: Wrath of the Mutants, Cruis'n Blast, and the aforementioned The Walking Dead: Streets of Survival. 

In a press release announcing the company, principal and head of partnerships Andy Koehler said the company's launch represents a belief in "what licensed gaming can become when talented teams and trusted partners collaborate behind a shared vision."

Related:Investment house Denmu announces 'auteur-first' $50 million fund for game devs

"We’ve always believed licensed games deserve the same creativity, care, and ambition as any major or indie release," he said. "Trailmark Games was built with exactly that philosophy."

About the Author

Columnist, Game Developer

Nicole Carpenter is a veteran reporter who has been covering the video game industry for over a decade. 



https://www.gamedeveloper.com/business/new-publisher-trailmark-games-promises-new-path-forward-for-licensed-games
